settings.heading: Fix Old Metal Building Insulation Without Tearing It Out
settings.lead: Cover torn, stained, or sagging insulation with a clean BlueTex finish using the TermBar system. Built for metal buildings, pole barns, shops, and hangars.
settings.strip: Blocks 97% radiant heat | Vapor barrier | 10× more durable than vinyl

settings.heading: The TermBar System Gives BlueTex Its Finished Look
settings.lead: Aluminum TermBars fasten BlueTex tight to the purlins and girts of the building. The result is a flat, drum-tight, finished panel look you can paint if you want.

settings.heading: Try the System Before You Commit to a Full Retrofit
settings.lead: The $100 BlueTex Cover/Repair System Sample Box has a full-size BlueTex piece, a TermBar section, seam tape, and fasteners — everything to test the system on one section of your building.

settings.heading: What It Is
settings.body: TermBars are aluminum bars that fasten the BlueTex material to the building frame.

settings.heading: Why It Works
settings.body: It creates a clean panel look and keeps the material tight against the purlins and girts.

settings.heading: Why It's Popular
settings.body: Most customers prefer the finished look and strong mechanical attachment.

settings.heading: Other Options
settings.body: EasyClip and other methods are secondary, used when the building calls for them.

settings.question: Is It Strong?
settings.answer: Yes. TermBars mechanically fasten the material to the building frame. The finished wall is tighter and more durable than the original metal building insulation it covers.

settings.question: Can You Paint It?
settings.answer: Yes. BlueTex and the TermBars both hold paint. Most customers leave it bright white for light reflection, but you can roll or spray any latex paint for a custom color.

settings.question: Do I Have to Tear Out the Old Insulation?
settings.answer: Usually no. BlueTex is commonly installed over existing insulation. The TermBar presses the old material in place and the new BlueTex creates the finished interior.
